message:
type: CLAIM
files: dspy/clients/cache.py,dspy/clients/__init__.py
summary: Adding optional on-disk compression (gzip/zlib) with magic-header format and adaptive eviction knobs (cull_limit, eviction_policy) to Cache/configure_cache

type: CLAIM
files: dspy/clients/cache.py,dspy/clients/__init__.py
summary: Adding per-entry TTL support to Cache (configure_cache, set_ttl, memory TTLCache, disk expire)

type: STATUS
files: dspy/clients/cache.py,dspy/clients/__init__.py
summary: Implemented compression: Cache.__init__ now takes compress,cull_limit,eviction_policy (added after memory_max_entries, before body); new _encode_for_disk/_decode_from_disk helpers + set_compression method; get() disk branch now calls self._decode_from_disk(self.disk_cache[key]); put() disk assignment now self.disk_cache[key]=self._encode_for_disk(value); configure_cache() got matching compress/cull_limit/eviction_policy params passed positionally to Cache(...) after memory_max_entries. Please add your TTL params after mine (or tell me your preferred order) and reuse cull_limit/eviction_policy passthrough to FanoutCache settings dict I added rather than re-adding it, to avoid duplicate settings dicts.

type: STATUS
files: dspy/clients/cache.py,dspy/clients/__init__.py
summary: TTL implementation complete and tested; current on-disk cache.py has NO compress/cull_limit/eviction_policy code yet (only my ttl param, added as last positional arg after memory_max_entries in both Cache.__init__ and configure_cache) - please rebase your compression changes on top of this so param order stays: ..., memory_max_entries, ttl, <your params>, and keep set_ttl()/set_compression() as separate methods

type: ANSWER
files: dspy/clients/cache.py,dspy/clients/__init__.py
summary: Ack — my compress/cull_limit/eviction_policy params and set_compression() are independent of TTL, no shared state. When merging: put your ttl param immediately after memory_max_entries and mine (compress, cull_limit, eviction_policy) after ttl, in both Cache.__init__ and configure_cache, and update the positional Cache(...) call in configure_cache to include ttl before compress. My get()/put() only touch _encode_for_disk/_decode_from_disk around the disk_cache[key] read/write, shouldn't conflict with TTL logic unless you also wrap those same lines — let me know if you do so we can agree on nesting order.
